What does the following CSS selector select?
h2:hover { ... }
answers: it selects all the "h2" elements when the user double clicks them
it selects all the "h2" elements when the user clicks them
it selects all the "h2" elements when the mouse goes over them
it selects all the "h2" elements when the user right-clicks them

question:
What does the following CSS selector select?
div[data-type^="content"] { ... }
answers: it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" whose value contains "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" whose value starts with "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" whose value does not contain "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" whose value ends with "content"
correct: it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" whose value starts with "content"

question:
What does the following CSS selector select?
div[data-type*="content"] { ... }
answers: it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" which contains the value "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" with its value ending with "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" with its value starting with "content"
it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" which does not contain the value "content"
correct: it selects all the "div" elements that have the attribute "data-type" which contains the value "content"
